name: Chou-Chou Koot/Curry/Pooduthual
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the skin of the squash.
2. Cube the squash.
3. Cook the cubes with a little water, turmeric, and salt until they are done but still firm.
4. Add the coconut and green chili mixture.
5. Pour the seasoning on top. If there is more liquid, it becomes kootu; if moist but not liquidy, then poduthual.
Variations:
- Use plaintains (raw, green, or very green) for a vazhakai poduthual/thoran.
- Use red pumpkin/butternut squash similarly.
- An excellent and quick chakka thoran is also possible.
- A Thai canned product of green jackfruit, already cooked, can be used. Shred it by hand, prepare the seasoning in 5-6 tablespoons of oil, and toss in crushed coconut and green chillies with salt. Stir well until thoroughly heated through and still moist. Fresh coconut is preferred.
